title: United Super Pty Ltd (trustee for Cbus) — Regulatory action
investigation_objective: APRA accepted a court enforceable undertaking from United Super Pty Ltd, trustee for the Construction and Building Unions Superannuation Fund (Cbus, over 920,000 member accounts and around $100 billion in funds under management), after a prudential review found significant and persistent weaknesses in its operational risk management framework across four areas: operational risk management, information/IT resilience, insurance claims processing and administration, and expenditure management. APRA separately launched an investigation into United Super for possible breaches of the Superannuation Industry (Supervision) Act 1993, focused on expenditure management practices. In a separate ASIC action (25-286MR), the Federal Court ordered United Super to pay a $23.5 million penalty after admitting serious failures in processing death benefit and total-and-permanent-disability insurance claims, which delayed payments to more than 7,000 members, plus two failures to report the matters to ASIC within required statutory timeframes. United Super was separately ordered to undertake a compliance program, pay roughly $500,000 towards ASIC's legal costs, and fund an approximately $32 million member remediation program for lost earnings and wrongfully charged fees.
scope: Subject of an APRA court enforceable undertaking following findings of significant and persistent operational risk management weaknesses; Undertaking spans four areas: operational risk management, IT resilience, insurance claims administration, and expenditure management; Separately under APRA investigation for possible breaches of the SIS Act relating to expenditure management practices; Ordered to pay a separate $23.5 million penalty for serious, admitted failures in processing members' death benefit and disability insurance claims, delaying payments to over 7,000 members; Admitted two failures to report the claims-processing failures to ASIC within required statutory timeframes
